title: SWIM Upgrade Failure Alerting

compliance.assurance_rationale: Failed software image or firmware operations can leave network devices in an unknown or half-upgraded state; rapid detection and response reduces incident impact.
compliance.controlObjective: Generates near-real-time notice when Catalyst Center reports SWIM or image update failures for managed devices so response can follow the IR playbook.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Alert history from UC-5.13.58 with `failureReason` and `deviceName`, linked to incident tickets and rollback or retry records in change tools.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Identifying and remediating failed image upgrades is part of an effective software flaw and patch management process for the managed network stack.
compliance.controlObjective: Surfaces which devices failed to take the target image so teams can re-run, schedule maintenance, or fall back in line with the organization’s update policy.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: List of failed upgrades by `deviceFamily` and `targetVersion` from Splunk, with follow-up work tracked to closure in the change system.

dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim`. via custom scripted input polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/compliance/detail?complianceType=IMAGE` (deploy per docs/guides/catalyst-center.md § Custom Scripted Inputs). Default poll interval: 3600s (1h). Volume: ~1 event per device per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `imageCompliance` (string), `runningVersion` (string), `targetVersion` (string), `deviceFamily` (string), `deviceName` (string), `managementIpAddress` (string), `upgradeStatus` (string). CIM data model: Change.
app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538) + custom scripted input for SWIM API
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim" upgradeStatus="FAILED" | stats count as failure_count values(failureReason) as reasons by deviceName, deviceFamily, runningVersion, targetVersion | sort -failure_count
description: Alerts when firmware upgrade operations fail, with the failure reason and affected device details for immediate troubleshooting.
value: Failed upgrades may leave devices in an inconsistent state. Immediate alerting ensures recovery actions (rollback, retry, manual intervention) start promptly.
implementation:
Extend the SWIM collector so each failed upgrade produces at least: `deviceName`, `deviceFamily`, `runningVersion`, `targetVersion`, `upgradeStatus=FAILED`, and `failureReason` (from API fields such as `failureReason`, `message`, or nested error objects in `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/network-device-image-updates` responses). Authenticate with `POST /dna/system/api/v1/auth/token`.

**Scripted input** (short interval during change windows, for example 300 s):

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_catalyst_swim/bin/collect_swim.py]
interval = 300
sourcetype = cisco:dnac:swim
index = catalyst
disabled = 0
```

**Event notifications (optional):** If your Catalyst Center can push SWIM failure events, configure a Platform webhook to Splunk HEC with `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim` and merge failure payloads into the same index for correlation. Ensure deduplication if both poll and webhook are active (use a stable `event_id` in the script/HEC payload).
visualization: Table (deviceName, failureReason, runningVersion, targetVersion), top failure reasons, trend of failure_count over time during upgrade windows.

references.title: Catalyst Center SWIM Compliance API — Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Transient image push failure that succeeds on automatic retry.** SWIM includes built-in retry logic. A transient network error during image download may fail on the first attempt but succeed on the retry. Distinguish by checking whether the same `deviceName` shows both FAILURE and SUCCESS within the upgrade window. Suppress by requiring the failure to persist as the final status (no subsequent SUCCESS) before alerting.

**Device unreachable during scheduled upgrade window.** A device that is powered off, rebooting, or network-isolated during the upgrade window will fail the SWIM push. This may be an expected condition (device in maintenance). Distinguish by correlating with `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth"` for `reachabilityHealth=Unreachable` on the same device. Suppress by checking device reachability before escalating SWIM failures.

**Insufficient flash storage on the target device.** Older devices with limited flash storage may fail the image download because there is not enough space for the new image. Distinguish by checking the SWIM failure message for "insufficient space" or similar storage-related errors. Do not suppress — this requires manual intervention to free storage or replace the device.

**Incompatible firmware version for the device's hardware revision.** Some firmware versions do not support specific hardware revisions or line card types. The SWIM push will fail during pre-check validation. Distinguish by checking the SWIM failure message for compatibility-related errors. Do not suppress — select a compatible firmware version for the device.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with the **swim** modular input enabled to `index=catalyst`, `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim`.
• Active or recent SWIM upgrade campaigns with at least one device in FAILURE status.
• RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE**.
• Alert action configured for operations team notification (email, Slack, PagerDuty).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
SWIM upgrade failures are captured in the same SWIM API data. Failures can occur at multiple stages: image download, image distribution, pre-check validation, or firmware activation.

Key fields for failure alerting:
• `upgradeStatus`: "FAILURE" indicates an upgrade failure.
• `failureReason` or `errorMessage`: description of why the upgrade failed.
• `deviceName`: the device that failed.
• `taskId`: links the failure to a specific upgrade campaign.
• `distributionStatus`, `activationStatus`: which stage failed.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim" upgradeStatus="FAILURE"
| stats latest(failureReason) as failure_reason latest(distributionStatus) as dist_status latest(activationStatus) as act_status count as failure_events by deviceName, deviceFamily, platformId, taskId
| eval failure_stage=case(dist_status="FAILURE", "Distribution", act_status="FAILURE", "Activation", 1==1, "Unknown")
| sort failure_stage, deviceFamily
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`upgradeStatus="FAILURE"`**: Filters to only failed upgrade attempts.
• **`latest(failureReason)`**: Gets the most recent failure reason per device — a device may have multiple failure events if the upgrade was retried.
• **`failure_stage`**: Identifies whether the failure occurred during image distribution (download to device) or activation (boot onto new image). Distribution failures are typically recoverable; activation failures may require manual intervention.

For alerting: save as a scheduled search running every 15 minutes. Trigger on `count > 0`. Route to the network operations team.

Step 3 — Validate
• **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > Design > Image Repository > Tasks. Filter by FAILURE status. Compare the failed device list with the Splunk results.
• **Failure reason check:** Verify the failure reasons match between Catalyst Center and Splunk — some reasons are truncated in the API response.
• **Vendor UI parity:** cross-reference the Splunk results with the corresponding **Catalyst Center > Assurance** page for the same time window to confirm counts and values match.
Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Failed upgrade summary table with device name, failure stage, failure reason, and platform. Add a trend chart of failures over time.
• **Alert:** Trigger immediately on any FAILURE event. Include `deviceName`, `failure_stage`, and `failure_reason` in the alert notification for quick triage.
• **Retry workflow:** After addressing the failure cause, retry the upgrade from Catalyst Center > Design > Image Repository > Tasks.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Transient failure followed by success:** Check whether the same `deviceName` has both FAILURE and SUCCESS events for the same `taskId`. If so, the retry succeeded and the failure can be acknowledged.
• **Flash space failure:** The device doesn't have enough flash for the new image. Free space by removing old images: `delete /force flash:old_image.bin` from the device CLI.
• **Distribution failure — device unreachable:** The device was offline during the download window. Verify device reachability and retry.
• **Activation failure — incompatible configuration:** Some configurations are not supported on the new firmware version. Check the Cisco release notes for configuration compatibility.
• **No SWIM events arriving:** SWIM requires a custom scripted input; verify the script is deployed and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages.
grandmaExplanation: We immediately flag any device that failed to update its software, along with the reason why, so your team can fix it before it causes problems.
